published_prior_art.cite: ManskiTamer2002 and BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 give the interval/random-set grammar; no learned-representation selector LP.
BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 gives general random-set partial identification; the representation-fiber selector semantics are not stated there.
ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments and CorcollAndreuVlontzosORiordanGilliganLee2026 use exact/causal-latent representation assumptions, not a bounded-distortion audit equivalence.
Routine interval singleton algebra under the proposed B_epsilon functional.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the current flagship kernel is mostly the singleton/frontier collapse of a random-set envelope plus GenAI endpoint framing, and needs a genuinely nontrivial generic-class obstruction or new audit theory to clear a flagship floor.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The iff is too broad for any selector-free E-dagger: the setup allows E-dagger to post-process P and mu-dagger and ignore uncalibrated point values, so mu-dagger endpoint calibration is not necessary unless the rule class is narrowed to literal endpoint/single-surface evaluations.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Narrow Conjecture 2's endpoint-rule class or replace the mu-dagger calibration condition with a condition on the actual reported E-dagger functional.

Add a quantitative generic-class theorem: specify an open set of observable laws and target fibers where every admissible selector-free endpoint has a lower-bounded worst-case error or sign-failure radius.

Separate the routine BMM/Manski envelope claims from the representation-learning contribution by making Theorem 1 and Theorem 2 explicitly auxiliary, with Conjecture 2 carrying the only flagship claim.

recommended_next_step: Revise the flagship audit theorem to fix the endpoint-rule quantifier and add a nontrivial generic-class obstruction strong enough for the flagship novelty floor.
